{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

What you'll learn

  • Thing one
  • Thing two
  • Thing three

Prerequisites

  • Curiosity
  • A browser

Somewhere between the brief and the deadline lives the actual work. This is generic demo content so you can judge the layout honestly.

The setup

Every good system starts with a constraint: frame the color 1:1, crop out the noise.

  • Start with the audience, not the tool
  • Measure twice, publish once
  • Delete more than you add

The details

const frame = (life) => life.filter(m => m.signal > m.noise);
Life looks better when you frame the color 1:1.

Wrap up

If this reads well in all six theme modes, ship it.

"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0], [1, "p", []]]}

posts.title: Java -- University Course (50 Lessons)
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Java -- University Course (50 Lessons) is a hands-on course with real-time, real-world code examples in every lesson, not toy snippets.

Real-time example

This course walks through Java -- University Course (50 Lessons) the way it's actually used on the job -- production-style examples over abstract theory.

-- start here, then work through the lessons in order

What's happening

Each lesson below stands alone but builds on the ones before it -- concept, a real-time example, working code, and a takeaway.

Work through all 50 lessons in order for the full course.
"}]], "markups": [], "sections": [[10, 0], [1, "p", []]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: 50 lessons covering Java from the JVM to Spring Boot, each with a real-time, real-world example and working code -- not toy snippets.
